title: Former LDP senator sentenced to 600,000 yen fine for fund scandal
summary: The Tokyo court found Yasumasa Ono guilty of omitting income in the ruling party's illegal funding case.

title: U.S. and Iran advance in negotiations to ease oil sanctions
summary: Both parties agreed to a 60-day truce on Iranian crude oil sanctions and continue dialogue on the nuclear program.

title: Japan's Finance Minister and U.S. Treasury Secretary discuss currencies
summary: Katayama and Bessent agreed on the need to act decisively in the face of speculative movements in the foreign exchange market.

title: Japan commemorates the 81st anniversary of the Battle of Okinawa with prayers for peace
summary: Citizens and Governor Denny Tamaki remember the fallen and reaffirm commitment to a peaceful society.

title: Yen weakens to 161 yen per dollar, its lowest level in two years
summary: The Japanese currency continues to depreciate amid expectations of government intervention to halt the decline.

title: Messi becomes all-time top scorer in World Cups with two goals
summary: The Argentine scored two goals to reach 18 in World Cups, surpassing Klose's record, and qualified Argentina for the round of 16.

title: Ohtani hits his seventeenth home run and closes in on 300 in MLB
summary: The Japanese started the game with a homerun as leadoff batter and is three hits away from the historic mark.

title: United Kingdom heads for a new prime minister after Johnson's resignation
summary: The Labour Party will elect its leader unopposed, and he is expected to take office in mid-July.

title: Prices of used apartments in Tokyo reach record 128 million yen
summary: The average price in May rose 27% year-on-year, although the city center shows signs of stagnation.

title: France defeats Iraq in a match with the first interruption of the World Cup
summary: The Group I match was temporarily stopped due to incidents in the stadium, but the French managed the victory.

title: Ohtani leads fan voting for the MLB All-Star Game
summary: The Dodgers player maintains first place in the popular vote for the Major League Baseball All-Star.

title: The emperors of Japan visit Belgium and meet with King Philippe and Queen Mathilde
summary: The imperial couple stayed at the Palace of Laeken and highlighted the cultural and historical ties between both countries.

title: Alan Greenspan, legendary former chairman of the U.S. Federal Reserve, dies
summary: Greenspan, known as 'the maestro' for his economic management, died at 98.

title: Norway defeats Senegal with double from Erling Haaland in the World Cup
summary: The striker scored two goals to give his team the victory in Group I.

title: Nikkei index falls more than 500 points on profit taking
summary: The Tokyo stock market fell to 71,800 yen in a session of high volatility.

title: Body found in a freezer in Kobe; may be of a resident who died in 2011
summary: Authorities investigate the unidentified body found in an apartment, with no belongings that facilitate its identification.

title: Woman sentenced to 27 years in prison for murder of student in Hokkaido
summary: The accused was found guilty of killing a 17-year-old girl in the city of Rumoi.

title: Explosion at one of the world's largest liquefied gas plants in Qatar leaves 13 dead
summary: The blast at the Ras Laffan plant caused 66 injured and serious material damage.

title: Removal of debris from hydrogen explosion at Fukushima Daiichi begins
summary: The operation at unit 1 of the nuclear power plant begins 15 years after the 2011 accident.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi will not attend NATO summit for fear of losing influence
summary: The decision not to participate in the Atlantic alliance meeting raises concerns about the weakening of Japanese leadership.
